questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An article costing $200$ is sold for $250$. The profit percentage is _____ % .

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The bar chart shows four values: $10, 20, 30, 40$. Compute their total.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='100' width='25' height='20'/><rect x='85' y='80' width='25' height='40'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='40' width='25' height='80'/></g><text x='50' y='138' font-size='8'>10, 20, 30, 40</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An amount of $600$ is divided in the ratio $2:3$. The larger part is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each share equals the total times its ratio fraction.

**Working.** Larger fraction $=\dfrac{3}{2+3}=\dfrac{3}{5}$, so
$$600\times\frac{3}{5}=360$$

**Answer.** $360$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of $10, 20, 30, 40$ and $50$ is _____ .

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The simple interest on $5000$ at $8\%$ per annum for $3$ years is _____ .

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at exactly $4{:}00$ is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Angle $=30^\circ\times$ (hours between hands).

**Working.** At $4{:}00$ the hands are $4$ marks apart: $4\times 30^\circ = 120^\circ$.

**Answer.** $120$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If $12$ workers build a wall in $10$ days, how many days will $15$ workers (of equal efficiency) take to build the same wall?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Workers and time are inversely proportional for a fixed task.

**Working.**
$$\text{days}=\frac{12\times 10}{15}=8$$

**Answer.** $8$ days.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The lowercase letter shown, rotated $180^\circ$ in the plane, looks like the letter

figure.caption: The lowercase letter b.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><text x='35' y='88' font-size='80' fill='#334155'>b</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A $180^\circ$ rotation flips both top-bottom and left-right.

**Working.** 'b' rotated $180^\circ$ becomes 'q'.

**Answer.** q.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A town's population of $8000$ grows by $5\%$ each year. After $2$ years the population is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Compound growth multiplies by $(1.05)$ each year.

**Working.** $8000\times(1.05)^2=8000\times1.1025=8820$.

**Answer.** $8820$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A price is first increased by $10\%$ and then decreased by $10\%$. The net percentage change in the price is _____ % (use a negative sign for a decrease).

questions.subject: Palaeontology
questions.stem: Foraminifera are widely used in petroleum biostratigraphy because they are
options: abundant calcareous microfossils with rapid evolution
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Forams are abundant, environmentally sensitive, fast-evolving microfossils.

**Working.**
Calcareous microfossils.

**Answer.** Calcareous microfossils

questions.stem: A gravity station is $1000\,$m above the datum. The free-air correction is _____ mGal.

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $30^\circ$ and dips $35^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=35^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $35^\circ$

questions.subject: Mathematics & Geostatistics
questions.stem: Earthquakes occur as a Poisson process with mean $\lambda=3$ per year. The probability of zero events in a year is _____.

questions.subject: Economic & Ore Geology
questions.stem: Chromite and platinum-group deposits typically form by
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Chromite/PGE concentrate by magmatic (cumulate) segregation in mafic–ultramafic layered intrusions.

**Working.**
Magmatic segregation.

**Answer.** Magmatic segregation

questions.subject: Engineering & Environmental Geology
questions.stem: Rock Quality Designation (RQD) is computed from drill core as the percentage of
options: intact pieces ≥10 cm summed over the run length
questions.solution:
**Concept.** RQD = Σ(intact pieces ≥10 cm) / total core run × 100.

**Working.**
Pieces ≥10 cm.

**Answer.** Σ pieces ≥10 cm

questions.subject: Structural Geology

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=35^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $35^\circ$

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A reverse fault forms under a stress regime in which
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Andersonian theory: reverse (thrust) faulting has horizontal $\sigma_1$, vertical $\sigma_3$.

**Working.**
Horizontal $\sigma_1$.

**Answer.** $\sigma_1$ horizontal
